economic_notes: Band is contingently liable as covenantor for Kwoiek Creek Resources Limited Partnership term loan up to $3,100,000. Teck HVC Partnership provides significant revenue. Devastating wildfire in late June 2021 destroyed 90% of Lytton homes and businesses (subsequent event). COVID-19 pandemic noted as ongoing risk. Band receives 41% of revenue from ISC Comprehensive Funding Arrangement.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2021 but management responsibility letter references March 31, 2020 — appears to be a document error. Three GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis: Kanaka Bar Land and Resources Limited Partnership (99%), Kanaka Bar Land and Resources Inc. (100%), and KBES Services LLP (99%). Five segments fully consolidated. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,238,676 + Province of BC $659,920 + First Nations Health Authority $342,239 + Government of Canada $301,763 + First Nations Emergency Services Society $156,630 + CMHC $33,712 + BC Hydro $14,184 (treated as government) = $3,746,739; OSR = $5,316,187 - $3,746,739 = $1,569,448. Long-term debt = term loan $725,041 + CMHC mortgage $21,330 = $746,371. Annual debt service = CMHC mortgage repayment $11,248 (term loan was interest-only during the year). Remuneration schedule not included in financial statements provided. Significant subsequent event: Lytton wildfire destroyed 90% of community structures in late June 2021.
